title: elymus multiflorus subsp. kingianus
intro: Elymus multiflorus subsp. kingianus, also known as King's wildrye, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for wounds and skin infections. The health benefits of Elymus multiflorus subsp. kingianus include reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. Elymus multiflorus subsp. kingianus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for topical or internal use, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal usage.
